name: PHYLLODES TUMOR
definition: FIBROADENOMA PHYLLODES = GIANT FIBROADENOMA benign giant form of intracanalicular fibroadenoma PHYLLODES SARCOMA = CYSTOSARCOMA PHYLLODES = ADENOSARCOMA rare fibroepithelial neoplasia

demographics.Prevalence: 1÷6,300 examinations; 0.3-1.5% of all breast neoplasms; 3% of all fibroadenomas
demographics.Mean age: 45 years (5th-6th decade); occasionally in women < 20 years of age
demographics.Histo: similar to fibroadenoma but with increased cellularity + pleomorphism of its stromal

discriminating_features: large homogeneous noncalcified isodense mass with smooth polylobulated margins
rapid growth to large size (> 6-10 cm), may fill entire breast
occasional "halo" sign ← compression of surrounding tissue
hypointense regions of cystic / necrotic changes (occasionally)

general: large homogeneous noncalcified isodense mass with smooth polylobulated margins
rapid growth to large size (> 6-10 cm), may fill entire breast
occasional "halo" sign ← compression of surrounding tissue
US: round / lobulated hypoechoic mass with smooth margins
heterogeneous internal echoes without acoustic shadowing
anechoic fluid-filled clefts in large tumors (DDx from fibroadenoma)
MR: hypointense regions of cystic / necrotic changes (occasionally)
strong initial uptake in solid tumor component
continuous increase / plateau in postinitial phase
increasing demarcation of nonenhancing cystic components (mass-in-mass morphology)
iso - to hyperintense to breast parenchyma
hyperintense regions of cystic / necrotic changes (occasionally) = "slit-like" cystic
type III washout pattern in solid tumor portion

clinical.Rx: wide excision (without axillary node dissection); radiation + chemotherapy not useful
